english: pe tsa* (pe tsha) manufactured salt, *zó-pe ser* (bzo pe gser) artificial gold Wdn. -- bzó-bo (bzo bo) = bzó-pa (bzo pa), bzó-bo mkás-pa (bzo bo mkhas pa) a skilful artist Mil.; bzo-byéd (bzo byed) 1. id. 2. imaginative faculty, imagination, ni f. -- bzo-lás (bzo las) work Sch.

(rarely bzád-pa (bzad pa)) I. vb., क्षम, 1. to suffer, bear, endure, c. acc., mig ná-ba ma bzód-nas (mig na ba ma bzod nas) not being able to bear the pain in his eyes Dzl.; lus ,dis na mi bzod (lus 'dis na mi bzod) with this body pain, disease, cannot be endured Thgy.; saṅs-rgyás-kyi túgs-rǰe čé-bas ma bzód-nas (sangs rgyas kyi thugs rje che bas ma bzod nas) seems to imply: Buddha in his mercy not suffering this, but checking the mischief; — also c. dat.: ǰám-po-la mi bzod ('jam po la mi bzod) he cannot bear what is soft or smooth Dzl.; ma-bdé-ba bág-tsam-la bzód-pa mi byéd-de (ma bde ba bag tsam la bzod pa mi byed de) getting so fretful through a slight indisposition Mil.; ltá-bas mi bzód-de (lta bas mi bzod de) finding it unbearable for his eyes Pth.; drán-pas mi bzód-de (dran pas mi bzod de) as much as: so that he almost lost his senses over it Pth.; bzód-tabs (bzod thabs) (or bzod-glags (bzod glags))-méd-par °byúṅ-ba (med par 'byung ba) or °gyúr-ba ('gyur ba) not to be able to bear ... any longer, frq.; mi-bzód-pa (mi bzod pa) or -bzád-pa (bzad pa) adj., unbearable, intolerable, also irresistible; ma bzód-nas (ma bzod nas) not being able to resist any longer Dzl. — 2. to forgive, pardon, sṅan-čad °to-tsám-pa bzód-par ysol (sngan chad 'tho tsam pa bzod par gsol) to pardon our former tricks is what we beg Mil.; rtá-la ma skyón-pa bzód-par bžes °tsal (rta la ma skyon pa bzod par bzhes 'tshal) that I did not request you to mount, this I beg you to forgive me Mil.; bzód-par rsól-lo byas kyaṅ (bzod par gsol lo byas kyang) although she begged pardon Pth.; skyón-rnams yé-šes-spyan-ldan-rnams-la bzód-par ysol (skyon rnams ye shes spyan ldan rnams la bzod par gsol) with respect to the deficiencies I pray for the indulgence of the very wise (readers); bzod-ysól byéd-pa (bzod gsol byed pa) to ask pardon, forbearance Pth. — II. sbst. 1. patience (Ssk. क्षान्ति), bzód-pa sgóm-pa (bzod pa sgom pa) to exercise one's self in patience Dzl. v, 12; but also, to have patience, to show forbearance; bzód-pa bžés-pa (bzod pa bzhes pa), id. resp. (v. also above I, 2); bzód-pa-čan (bzod pa can) patient; bzod-sráṅ (bzod srang) unwearied patience; bzod-pa-čuṅ (bzod pa chung) impatient Mil., bzod-med (bzod med) Cs. id. — 2. in asceticism: perseverance, stedfast adherence to the four truths, constancy in pursuing the path that has been entered upon, mi skye-bai čos-la bzod-pa (mi skye ba'i chos la bzod pa) acc. to Was. id., being at the same time no longer subject to rebirths, p. (140). — Observ. So far as 'to forgive' implies patience, forbearance, it may be rendered by bzód-pa (bzod pa); but as the Scriptural view of 'forgiveness of sin' involves more than that, other expressions, such as bú-lon sél-ba (bu lon sel ba), must be resorted to with reference to the latter.
tub, carried on the back, to convey water, v. ču-bzóm (chu bzom) sub ču (chu).

(cf. zlá-ba (zla ba) II, 2); zlá-ba (zla ba) in a strict sense, is stated to be the silent, brǰód-pa (brjod pa) the soft, yet audible pronouncing of spells etc., bzlas-brǰód (bzlas brjod) signifying both together; bzlas-brǰód byéd-pa (bzlas brjod byed pa) to mutter over Glr.; mú-stegs-pai bzlas-brǰód (mu stegs pa'i bzlas brjod) Brahmanical spell-murmuring Thgy.
the contrary, the reverse, prá-ba-las bzlog sbóm-po (phra ba las bzlog sbom po) the contrary of thin is thick Lex.

1. a letter peculiar to the Tibetan language, which, contrary to ཨ (a) (q.v.) denotes the pure vowel, without any admixture of a consonant sound. The difficulty which attaches to the articulation of this vowel, requiring an opening of the glottis
